what_it_does: A static, high-fidelity desktop information-architecture mockup (1440px fixed-width) for a proposed 'commander's bridge' UI. It renders a three-zone layout — collapsible left palette, central node board, right inspector/address-help/queue rail — plus a docked bottom RHM conversation pane, using a gold-themed design system and data-ui-ref addressing to show how a selected 'ask' node is visible simultaneously across board, inspector, and chat.
observations: Self-contained HTML/CSS/JS file with no external build step
Uses a fixed 1440px viewport and CSS grid for the main 'bridge' layout
Top toolbar groups related verbs into labeled pill clusters rather than a flat row
Left rail is a collapsible palette of draggable node chips (process/content/config-slot kinds)
Central board shows node cards connected by SVG wires, with state-based color skins and a selected gold ring
Right rail is split into Inspector, Address-help, and Queue/Review regions
Inspector shows selected-node config slots, rendered output, and a 'grow the interface itself' affordance
Address-help uses progressive disclosure rows (chevron + count) for comments, history, versions, self-changes, code-behind
Queue/Review region surfaces build results, decisions, and questions as actionable cards
Bottom RHM pane has a presence orb, conversation about the selected node, and a 'tune' strip for /api/knobs, run-stats sparkline, and activity feed
Many elements carry data-ui-ref attributes using a ui://... address scheme
Minimal JS handles rail collapse, disclosure toggles, teach spotlight, and visual node selection only
Uses Google Fonts (Fraunces, IBM Plex Mono/Sans) and heavy CSS custom properties
Static context menu and hover-preview bubble are absolutely positioned near the selected node
standards: The simultaneity concept: one selected node address visible in board, inspector, and RHM at once
Surface-everything approach: latent capabilities collapsed into chips/counts/sparklines instead of deep menus
Docked RHM conversation pane with a co-presence indicator tied to the selected ui:// address
First-class 'grow the interface itself' affordance inside the inspector
Address-help as a progressively-disclosed 'what can I do here?' locus
Queue/Review as a permanent, in-view inbox column
Gold-themed 'living instrument' aesthetic with aurora, orb, grain, and Fraunces display type
conventions: Follows a design-system token set (colors, spacing, radii, elevation) via CSS custom properties
Uses semantic-ish HTML5 elements (main, aside, section) and data-* attributes for UI references
Class naming is compact and component-scoped (e.g., .node, .chip, .region, .turn)
Comments embed design rationale and precedent references directly in the file
Real-but-latent capabilities are named by plausible routes/paths (/api/knobs, /api/run-stats, panel/extend, annotations/chats, self-change-log, voice trial debrief)
Single-file mockup with inline styles and scripts, suitable for direct browser open
concerns: Fixed 1440px width is not responsive and may not fit smaller viewports
Node state is communicated primarily by color, which is an accessibility risk without additional text/aria cues
Static context menu and preview bubble positions may not align if layout or selection changes
No keyboard interaction or ARIA roles/labels despite complex interactive regions
Node selection JS only updates the visual ring; it does not re-bind inspector or RHM content
Heavy reliance on external Google Fonts for a mockup
Hardcoded fictional sample data contradicts the 'NO FICTION' claim in a literal sense, though it is framed as real-but-latent capability placeholders
notes: A sight-first desktop IA prototype that threads a selected 'ask' node through board, inspector, and RHM conversation to demonstrate coherence before any real build.
questions: How would the simultaneity binding actually update inspector and RHM content when a different node is selected?
What is the real backend contract for /api/knobs and /api/run-stats?
How are ui:// addresses resolved and persisted across the interface?
What is the approval/reversal flow for the 'grow the interface itself' self-change affordance?
Is there a responsive or mobile variant of this layout?
How will accessibility requirements (screen-reader state announcements, keyboard navigation) be met?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a target mockup for stakeholders to react by sight before the real build, rendering the proposed desktop IA. The file matches that purpose as a static visual prototype, but it remains a front-end shell without real data binding or backend integration.
apparent_themes: Simultaneity and coherence across three UI zones
Surface latent capabilities without creating deep junk-drawers
intention_signals: The file is trying to demonstrate a desktop IA where a single selected subject remains legible across the board, inspector, and conversation at once, while keeping advanced capabilities visible but collapsed so the interface feels powerful yet uncluttered and alive.
novelty: The persistent docked RHM pane with an 'indicate' chip showing the selected ui:// address, the first-class 'grow the interface itself' affordance in the inspector, the address-help progressive-disclosure family tied to ui:// loci, and the ambient tune strip that surfaces /api/knobs and run-stats without leaving the main view.
contribution: A concrete visual reference for a three-pane desktop IA that binds a selected node across board, inspector, and conversation, and that surfaces latent capabilities through progressive disclosure rather than hidden menus — useful as a layout, interaction, and addressed-UI vocabulary prototype.
summary_for_embedding: A static 1440px desktop information-architecture mockup for 'The Company' showing a gold-themed 'commander's bridge' with a grouped toolbar, collapsible left palette, central node board, right inspector/address-help/queue rail, and a docked RHM conversation pane, using data-ui-ref addressing to thread a selected ask node through all three zones.
intention_for_embedding: To let stakeholders preview and react to a proposed desktop IA that keeps a selected node coherent across board, inspector, and chat while surfacing latent capabilities as collapsed, recognizable affordances.
